stimulus: In 1893, German engineer Rudolf Diesel patented his new internal combustion engine. Diesel’s engine dramatically improved the fuel efficiency of the earlier compression engine, which the French engineer Alphonse Beau de Rochas ______blank two decades earlier, could achieve.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. Diesel patented his engine in 1893, and the earlier engine was designed “two decades earlier.” The past perfect “had designed” correctly indicates that one past event (the earlier design) occurred before another past event (Diesel’s patent).

Choices A, B, and D use present, future, and simple present tenses that don’t match the past time frame.
